features.description: Implement complete cleanup of workspace terminals when switching workspaces, including PTY process termination, xterm.js disposal, IPC listener removal, and ResizeObserver disconnection
preconditions: IPC handlers for terminal cleanup exist
TerminalCell component has useEffect cleanup pattern
expectedBehavior: All PTY processes killed when workspace switches
verificationSteps: Console output showing terminal cleanup logs
Task Manager screenshot showing PTY process count returns to baseline
Chrome DevTools Memory panel heap snapshot comparison
IPC handler logs showing cleanupAllTerminals execution
Manual: Switch between 10 workspaces and verify memory stability

features.description: Implement proper useEffect cleanup in TerminalCell component to dispose all xterm.js resources, addons, ResizeObserver, IPC listeners, and debounce timers on unmount
preconditions: TerminalCell component uses xterm.js with addons
ResizeObserver is used for responsive layout
verificationSteps: Console logs '[TerminalCell {id}] Cleaning up terminal' on unmount
React DevTools component tree shows clean unmount
Chrome DevTools Performance tab shows no orphaned listeners
Code review of useEffect cleanup function
Manual: Remove terminal and verify no errors

features.description: Implement proper IPC event listener cleanup for terminal lifecycle events including onTerminalData, onTerminalStarted, onTerminalExit, and onTerminalError
preconditions: IPC event listeners stored in refs
expectedBehavior: removeListener called for each IPC channel
verificationSteps: Console output showing unsubscribe calls
Chrome DevTools Memory panel showing listener count
Code screenshot of listenersRef.current cleanup
Test: 20+ terminal spawn/dispose cycle without MaxListenersExceededWarning
Manual: Verify IPC listener count matches active terminal count

features.description: Implement proper xterm.js event handler disposal including onData, onScroll, custom key event handlers, texture atlas clearing, and WebGL context release
preconditions: xterm.js terminal instances created with event handlers
Terminal has onData and onScroll handlers
expectedBehavior: term.dispose() called before terminal ref nullification
verificationSteps: Console log showing dispose call sequence
Chrome DevTools Performance > Event Listeners panel
WebGL context count before/after disposal
Memory heap snapshot showing no terminal references
Manual: Verify no 'write to disposed terminal' errors

features.description: Implement resize debouncing with 100-200ms delay to prevent excessive IPC calls during rapid window resizing
preconditions: Terminal resize sends IPC to main process
Multiple resize events can fire rapidly
expectedBehavior: Only final resize dimensions trigger IPC call
terminal.resize() and ptyProcess.resize() called once per sequence
verificationSteps: Console shows single terminalResize IPC call per resize gesture
Chrome DevTools Performance tab shows debounce delay
Visual verification of text preservation during resize
IPC monitor showing debounced call pattern
Manual: Rapid resize and verify single IPC call

features.description: Configure xterm.js scrollback buffer limits (1000-5000 lines) to prevent memory bloat during extended terminal sessions
preconditions: xterm.js terminal configured with scrollback option
Terminal can produce large amounts of output
expectedBehavior: Memory usage stable during 10,000+ lines output
No memory growth during long sessions
verificationSteps: Code screenshot showing xterm.js configuration
Chrome DevTools Memory panel during extended output
Memory usage graph over 30-minute session
terminal.clear() before/after memory comparison
Manual: Output 10,000+ lines and verify memory stability

features.description: Implement Windows-specific process tree termination using taskkill /f /t to kill entire process tree including child processes
preconditions: Current kill method may leave orphaned processes
expectedBehavior: Process count returns to baseline within 2 seconds
verificationSteps: Code screenshot of Windows-specific kill logic
Task Manager screenshot before/after terminal kill
Process Monitor output showing process tree termination
Test: spawn/kill cycle with process count verification
Manual: Kill terminal with child processes and verify cleanup

features.description: Configure xterm.js to use WebGL renderer for optimal terminal rendering performance with hardware acceleration
preconditions: Terminal renders large amounts of output
expectedBehavior: DOM renderer NOT used as primary renderer
FPS remains above 50 during rapid output
verificationSteps: Code screenshot showing WebGL addon configuration
Chrome DevTools Rendering panel showing WebGL active
Performance tab FPS graph during heavy output test
Comparison test: DOM vs WebGL rendering performance
Manual: Output 1000+ lines/sec and verify FPS > 50

features.description: Implement proper Zustand store state management including cleanup on workspace deletion, atomic updates, and debounced persistence writes
preconditions: Terminals can be created and deleted
expectedBehavior: Clear terminal state when workspace deleted
No references to disposed terminal components
verificationSteps: useWorkspaceStore.getState() returns current state only
Console shows debounced save logs
electron-store data matches store state
Code review of Zustand store cleanup logic
Manual: Delete workspace and verify store cleanup

features.description: Implement command history limits in terminal history store with FIFO eviction policy to prevent unbounded array growth
expectedBehavior: Old commands evicted using FIFO when limit exceeded
verificationSteps: Code screenshot showing history limit enforcement
Memory usage graph during 1000+ command test
Terminal history store state inspection
Console log showing eviction events
Manual: Execute 1500+ commands and verify eviction

features.description: Optimize IPC communication patterns to use minimal data transfer, batching, and proper invoke patterns instead of send/on
preconditions: IPC communication between renderer and main
Large terminal state objects may be passed
expectedBehavior: Send terminal IDs instead of full objects
IPC payloads contain only primitive types and IDs
verificationSteps: Code screenshot of IPC handler signatures
Chrome DevTools Performance > IPC timeline
IPC payload inspection showing minimal data
Benchmark results for IPC call latency (<5ms)
Manual: Verify no large objects in IPC calls

features.description: Set up comprehensive test infrastructure with unit tests, component tests, E2E tests, and performance tests achieving >80% coverage
preconditions: Vitest or Jest available for unit testing
expectedBehavior: Unit tests: >80% coverage for stores, services, utilities
Component tests: >70% coverage for TerminalCell, WorkspaceTabBar
E2E tests: All critical user workflows covered
Performance tests: Memory leak detection tests included
verificationSteps: bun run test:coverage shows >80% line coverage
Test file structure showing organized test suites
CI pipeline configuration showing test execution
Sample test output for critical path tests
Manual: Run tests and verify coverage report

features.description: Implement memory profiling utilities including MemoryProfiler class for heap snapshots, IPC handlers for renderer queries, and periodic logging
expectedBehavior: Dev mode logs memory every 10 seconds
verificationSteps: Code screenshot of MemoryProfiler implementation
Console output showing periodic memory logs
Memory snapshot comparison output
IPC handler for memory queries
Manual: Take snapshots and compare

features.description: Implement automated test suite with memory leak detection tests for terminal spawn/dispose, workspace switching, long-running stability, and process orphan detection
verificationSteps: Test code for memory leak detection
Test output showing pass/fail criteria
CI pipeline showing test execution
Memory growth graph from automated test
Manual: Run tests and verify leak detection
